zone_6b_notes: Zone 6b requires long-day onion varieties (Candy, Stuttgarter, Yellow Sweet Spanish) — short-day types bulb too early without adequate top growth. Plant sets in early April, 4–6 weeks before last frost. Harvest when 50–75% of tops fall over naturally (late June to July). Cure in warm, dry, airy location for 3–4 weeks before storage. Missouri spring rains can cause downy mildew; plant in well-drained beds.

planting.best_planting_time: Sets planted early April; transplants from seed started indoors in January

water: consistent — 1 inch/week during bulb development; reduce as tops fall
